diagrams.title: Process State Diagram
diagrams.description: Five-state model showing all possible process state transitions
elements: Arrows showing transitions with labels like 'Scheduler dispatch', 'I/O request', 'I/O complete', 'Exit', 'Time slice expired'
diagrams.purpose: Understanding how processes move through lifecycle and when state transitions occur in operating systems

diagrams.title: Process Control Block Structure
diagrams.description: Data structure storing all information about a process
elements: Process State (new, ready, running, waiting, terminated)
Memory Management Info (base/limit registers, page tables)
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing what information OS maintains about each process to manage execution and resources

diagrams.title: Process Memory Layout
diagrams.description: Logical memory organization of a process in RAM
elements: Stack (grows down) - local variables, function calls, return addresses
diagrams.purpose: Understanding memory allocation for processes, how different data types are stored, and why stack and heap grow toward each other
